---
title: "Multiple PERMANOVA"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: " last edit `r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  html_document:
    code_folding: show
    number_sections: yes
    smart: no
    toc: yes
    df_print: paged
    toc_float: yes
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# load necessary libraries
```{r}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, phyloseq, magrittr, janitor, microbiome, microViz, knitr, lubridate, naniar, readxl, ggplot2, ggpubr, vegan)
```

## Simple PERMANOVA loop
```{r}
###Here I continue to clean up data so that all columns with either 0 or 1 factors is removed (cannot get significance with these), and also anything above 70 factors is removed (usually implies it is the date of data generation, which will not be significant, so this speeds up the PERMANOVA script). Furthermore, below I also removed anything with "formdt" as this is usually when the data are entered.


#Subset by Age first#
subset_phy_by_age <- function(phy, age_value) {
  stopifnot(inherits(phy, "phyloseq"))
  md <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy))
  if (!("Age" %in% names(md))) stop("Column 'Age' not found in sample_data().")

  Age <- md$Age
  if (is.factor(Age)) Age <- as.numeric(as.character(Age))
  if (is.character(Age)) Age <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Age))

  keep <- !is.na(Age) & (Age == age_value)
  if (!any(keep)) stop(sprintf("No samples found for Age == %s", age_value))

  phy_sub <- prune_samples(keep, phy)
  phy_sub <- prune_samples(sample_sums(phy_sub) > 0, phy_sub)
  if (nsamples(phy_sub) < 2) stop("Not enough samples after filtering (need ≥2).")
  phy_sub
}

#TSS phyloseq for Bray/Sorensen
phy_age12 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_tss, age_value = 12)
phy_age4 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_tss, age_value = 4)
phy_age0 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_tss, age_value = 0)

#Non-TSS phyloseq for Aitchison
phy_Ait_age12 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_prev_10O_10R, age_value = 12)
phy_Ait_age4 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_prev_10O_10R, age_value = 4)
phy_Ait_age0 <- subset_phy_by_age(phy_prev_10O_10R, age_value = 0)

### NEED TO REMOVE ZEROS AFTER FILTERING BY AGE SINCE SOME QUESTIONS MIGHT ONLY HAVE SINGLE AGE RESPONSE ###
norm_vals <- function(x,
                      na_like = c("", "na", "n/a", "n\\a", "none", "null", "unknown", "missing")) {
  if (is.factor(x)) x <- as.character(x)
  if (is.character(x)) {
    v <- trimws(tolower(x))
    v[v %in% na_like] <- NA
    return(v)
  }
  x
}



### Trying Katie's Loop again ###
var_anly <- function(var, indata, dist_name = NULL, dist_obj = NULL) {
  require(phyloseq)
  # build or accept distance
  if (!is.null(dist_name))  make_dist <- phyloseq::distance(indata, method = dist_name)
  if (!is.null(dist_obj))   make_dist <- dist_obj

  # cleanly pull sample_data column
  sd <- as(sample_data(indata), "data.frame")
  x  <- sd[[var]]
  keep <- !is.na(x) & x != ""
  # need >1 level to run PERMANOVA
  if (dplyr::n_distinct(x[keep], na.rm = TRUE) > 1) {
    ss.phy  <- prune_samples(rownames(sd)[keep], indata)
    ss.dist <- as.matrix(make_dist)[sample_names(ss.phy), sample_names(ss.phy)]
    # run adonis2 using the distance matrix variable 'ss.dist'
    permanova <- vegan::adonis2(as.formula(paste0("ss.dist ~ ", var)),
                                data = as.data.frame(sample_data(ss.phy)))
    # return a simple numeric vector (avoids list-columns later)
    return(c(n = sum(keep),
             R2 = unname(permanova$R2[[1]]),
             P  = unname(permanova$`Pr(>F)`[[1]])))
  } else {
    return(c(n = NA_real_, R2 = NA_real_, P = NA_real_))
  }
}

var_anly <- function(var, indata, dist_name = NULL, dist_obj = NULL) {
  require(phyloseq)
  require(vegan)

  ## 1) build or accept distance
  if (!is.null(dist_name)) make_dist <- phyloseq::distance(indata, method = dist_name)
  if (!is.null(dist_obj))  make_dist <- dist_obj

  ## 2) extract metadata
  sd <- as.data.frame(sample_data(indata))
  x  <- sd[[var]]

  ## 3) keep non-missing
  keep <- !is.na(x)

  ## guard: need >1 level
  if (length(unique(x[keep])) > 1) {

    ## subset phyloseq + distance
    ss.phy  <- prune_samples(rownames(sd)[keep], indata)
    ss.dist <- as.dist(as.matrix(make_dist)[sample_names(ss.phy),
                                            sample_names(ss.phy)])

    ## IMPORTANT: keep column name as 'x'
    md <- data.frame(x = x[keep])
    rownames(md) <- rownames(sd)[keep]

    ## run PERMANOVA
    permanova <- vegan::adonis2(
      ss.dist ~ x,
      data = md,
      permutations = 999
    )

    return(c(
      n  = sum(keep),
      R2 = unname(permanova$R2[1]),
      P  = unname(permanova$`Pr(>F)`[1])
    ))
  } else {
    return(c(n = NA_real_, R2 = NA_real_, P = NA_real_))
  }
}

.clr_transform <- function(phy, pseudocount = 0.5) {
  otu <- as(otu_table(phy), "matrix")
  # phyloseq stores samples as columns if taxa_are_rows = TRUE
  if (taxa_are_rows(phy)) otu <- t(otu)
  # add pseudocount, then CLR per sample (row)
  otu_ps <- otu + pseudocount
  clr_mat <- t(apply(otu_ps, 1, function(x) log(x / exp(mean(log(x))))))
  return(clr_mat)
}

# extend .make_dist to handle aitchison
.make_dist <- function(phy, method, pseudocount = 1e-6) {
  m <- tolower(method)
  if (m == "jaccard")
    return(phyloseq::distance(phy, "jaccard", binary = TRUE))
  if (m %in% c("sorensen", "sørensen", "sorensen-dice"))
    return(phyloseq::distance(phy, "bray", binary = TRUE))
  if (m == "aitchison") {
    clr_mat <- .clr_transform(phy, pseudocount = pseudocount)
    return(dist(clr_mat, method = "euclidean"))  # base R dist, returns dist object
  }
  phyloseq::distance(phy, method = method)
}

beta_diversity_loop <- function(phy,
                                dist_list,   # named list of precomputed distances
                                var_list     = NULL,
                                sort_within  = TRUE,
                                drop_na_p    = TRUE) {
  if (is.null(var_list)) var_list <- sample_variables(phy)

  out <- lapply(names(dist_list), function(dist_name) {
    res_mat <- t(sapply(var_list, var_anly,
                        indata   = phy,
                        dist_obj = dist_list[[dist_name]]))
    df <- as.data.frame(res_mat, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    df$Variable <- rownames(df)
    rownames(df) <- NULL
    df$n  <- as.numeric(df$n)
    df$R2 <- as.numeric(df$R2)
    df$P  <- as.numeric(df$P)
    if (drop_na_p)    df <- df[!is.na(df$P), , drop = FALSE]
    if (sort_within)  df <- df[order(df$P, df$R2, na.last = TRUE), , drop = FALSE]
    rownames(df) <- NULL
    df
  })

  names(out) <- names(dist_list)
  out
}



### Added  distance matrix storage where they are saved so that we do not need to rerun the beta-diversity loop each time ###

### PERMANOVA Age 0 RESULTS ###
dist_matrices_age0 <- list(
  bray      = .make_dist(phy_age0,     "bray"),
  sorensen  = .make_dist(phy_age0,     "sorensen"),
  aitchison = .make_dist(phy_Ait_age0, "aitchison")
)
results_PERMANOVA_age0 <- beta_diversity_loop(
  phy       = phy_age0,
  dist_list = dist_matrices_age0
)

### PERMANOVA Age 4 RESULTS ###
dist_matrices_age4 <- list(
  bray      = .make_dist(phy_age4,     "bray"),
  sorensen  = .make_dist(phy_age4,     "sorensen"),
  aitchison = .make_dist(phy_Ait_age4, "aitchison")
)
results_PERMANOVA_age4 <- beta_diversity_loop(
  phy       = phy_age4,
  dist_list = dist_matrices_age4
)

### PERMANOVA Age 12 RESULTS ###
dist_matrices_age12 <- list(
  bray      = .make_dist(phy_age12,     "bray"),
  sorensen  = .make_dist(phy_age12,     "sorensen"),
  aitchison = .make_dist(phy_Ait_age12, "aitchison")
)
results_PERMANOVA_age12 <- beta_diversity_loop(
  phy       = phy_age12,
  dist_list = dist_matrices_age12
)
###Add day of birth? MaAsLiN for differential abundance for ASV testing against disease status. 
```

#Multiple PERMANOVA analysis, including VIF and collinearity
```{r}
#These are FDR corrected variables for PERMANOVA significance
get_sig_vars <- function(results, p_thresh = 0.05, adjust = TRUE, method = "BH") {
  lapply(results, function(df) {
    df <- df[!is.na(df$P), ]
    if (adjust) df$P_adj <- p.adjust(df$P, method = method)
    p_col <- if (adjust) "P_adj" else "P"
    df$Variable[df[[p_col]] < p_thresh]
  })
}

### Collinearity function to make sure the variables are removed before running multiple PERMANOVA ###
check_collinearity <- function(var_list, phy, cor_thresh = 0.7, vif_thresh = 5,
                               auto_remove = TRUE) {
  require(car)
  
  sd <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy))
  md <- sd[, var_list, drop = FALSE]
  
  md <- as.data.frame(lapply(md, function(col) {
    if (is.logical(col) | is.character(col)) as.factor(col)
    else col
  }))
  md <- md[complete.cases(md), , drop = FALSE]
  
  cat("=== Samples after NA removal:", nrow(md), "===\n\n")
  
  to_remove <- character(0)  # tracks all flagged variables
  
  # ── 1. Single-level check ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  n_levels <- sapply(md, function(col) length(unique(col)))
  single   <- names(n_levels[n_levels < 2])
  if (length(single) > 0) {
    cat("⚠ Single-level variables (removing):\n")
    print(single)
    to_remove <- union(to_remove, single)
  } else {
    cat("✓ No single-level variables\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
  
  # remove single-level before further checks so they don't contaminate VIF/cor
  md <- md[, !colnames(md) %in% to_remove, drop = FALSE]
  
  # ── 2. Numeric correlation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  num_cols <- md[, sapply(md, is.numeric), drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(num_cols) > 1) {
    cat("=== Numeric correlations (|r| >", cor_thresh, "flagged) ===\n")
    cor_mat  <- round(cor(num_cols, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"), 2)
    print(cor_mat)
    
    high_cor <- which(abs(cor_mat) > cor_thresh & upper.tri(cor_mat), arr.ind = TRUE)
    if (nrow(high_cor) > 0) {
      cat("\n⚠ High correlations — removing second variable in each pair:\n")
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(high_cor))) {
        v1 <- rownames(cor_mat)[high_cor[i, 1]]
        v2 <- colnames(cor_mat)[high_cor[i, 2]]
        r  <- cor_mat[high_cor[i, 1], high_cor[i, 2]]
        cat(" ", v1, "<-->", v2, ": r =", r, "-- removing", v2, "\n")
        to_remove <- union(to_remove, v2)
      }
    } else {
      cat("✓ No high numeric correlations\n")
    }
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # ── 3. Cramér's V ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat_cols <- md[, sapply(md, is.factor), drop = FALSE]
  # exclude already-flagged
  cat_cols <- cat_cols[, !colnames(cat_cols) %in% to_remove, drop = FALSE]
  
  if (ncol(cat_cols) > 1) {
    cat("=== Cramér's V (>", cor_thresh, "flagged) ===\n")
    cat_names <- colnames(cat_cols)
    v_mat <- matrix(NA, ncol(cat_cols), ncol(cat_cols),
                    dimnames = list(cat_names, cat_names))
    for (i in seq_along(cat_names)) {
      for (j in seq_along(cat_names)) {
        if (i == j) { v_mat[i,j] <- 1; next }
        tbl <- table(cat_cols[[i]], cat_cols[[j]])
        chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tbl))$statistic
        n   <- sum(tbl)
        k   <- min(nrow(tbl), ncol(tbl))
        v_mat[i,j] <- round(sqrt(chi / (n * (k - 1))), 2)
      }
    }
    print(v_mat)
    
    high_v <- which(v_mat > cor_thresh & upper.tri(v_mat), arr.ind = TRUE)
    if (nrow(high_v) > 0) {
      cat("\n⚠ High Cramér's V — removing second variable in each pair:\n")
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(high_v))) {
        v1 <- rownames(v_mat)[high_v[i, 1]]
        v2 <- colnames(v_mat)[high_v[i, 2]]
        cat(" ", v1, "<-->", v2, ": V =", v_mat[high_v[i,1], high_v[i,2]],
            "-- removing", v2, "\n")
        to_remove <- union(to_remove, v2)
      }
    } else {
      cat("✓ No high categorical associations\n")
    }
    cat("\n")
  }
  
  # ── 4. VIF ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  cat("=== VIF (threshold:", vif_thresh, ") ===\n")
  md_clean <- md[, !colnames(md) %in% to_remove, drop = FALSE]
  md_num   <- as.data.frame(lapply(md_clean, function(x) as.numeric(as.factor(x))))
  md_num   <- md_num[complete.cases(md_num), ]
  
  tryCatch({
    response   <- colnames(md_num)[1]
    predictors <- colnames(md_num)[-1]
    fmla     <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))
    vif_vals <- car::vif(lm(fmla, data = md_num))
    vif_df   <- data.frame(Variable = names(vif_vals),
                           VIF      = round(vif_vals, 2),
                           Flag     = ifelse(vif_vals > vif_thresh, "⚠ REMOVE", "✓ keep"))
    vif_df <- vif_df[order(-vif_vals), ]
    print(vif_df, row.names = FALSE)
    
    high_vif <- names(vif_vals[vif_vals > vif_thresh])
    if (length(high_vif) > 0) {
      cat("\n⚠ High VIF variables (removing):\n")
      print(high_vif)
      to_remove <- union(to_remove, high_vif)
    } else {
      cat("✓ No high VIF variables\n")
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("VIF could not be computed:", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
  })
  
  # ── 5. Summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  clean_vars <- var_list[!var_list %in% to_remove]
  cat("\n=== Summary ===\n")
  cat("Started with:  ", length(var_list), "variables\n")
  cat("Removed:       ", length(to_remove), "variables\n")
  if (length(to_remove) > 0) {
    cat("  Removed:", paste(to_remove, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }
  cat("Remaining:     ", length(clean_vars), "variables\n")
  cat("  Kept:", paste(clean_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  return(invisible(list(
    clean_vars = clean_vars,
    removed    = to_remove
  )))
}

### Now rerun the multiple PERMANOVA with the reduced list of vairables to test ###
multi_permanova <- function(var_list, indata, dist_obj, permutations = 999) {
  require(vegan)

  sd   <- as.data.frame(sample_data(indata))
  md   <- sd[, var_list, drop = FALSE]
  keep <- complete.cases(md)

  if (sum(keep) < 3) {
    warning("Too few complete cases after NA removal.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  ss.phy  <- prune_samples(rownames(sd)[keep], indata)
  ss.dist <- as.dist(as.matrix(dist_obj)[sample_names(ss.phy),
                                         sample_names(ss.phy)])

  md_sub <- as.data.frame(lapply(md[keep, , drop = FALSE], function(col) {
    if (is.logical(col) | is.character(col)) as.factor(col)
    else col
  }), row.names = rownames(md)[keep])

  safe_names <- paste0("var", seq_along(var_list))
  name_map   <- setNames(var_list, safe_names)
  colnames(md_sub) <- safe_names

  fmla <- as.formula(paste("ss.dist ~", paste(safe_names, collapse = " + ")))

  permanova <- vegan::adonis2(fmla, data = md_sub,
                              permutations = permutations, by = "margin")

  rownames(permanova) <- ifelse(
    rownames(permanova) %in% names(name_map),
    name_map[rownames(permanova)],
    rownames(permanova)
  )

  return(permanova)
}

run_multi_permanova <- function(sig_vars,
                                phy,
                                dist_results,
                                permutations = 999,
                                min_vars = 2) {
  out <- lapply(names(sig_vars), function(dist_name) {
    vars <- sig_vars[[dist_name]]

    if (length(vars) < min_vars) {
      message(dist_name, ": fewer than ", min_vars, " significant vars, skipping.")
      return(NULL)
    }

    message("Running multi-PERMANOVA for: ", dist_name,
            " | Variables: ", paste(vars, collapse = ", "))

    multi_permanova(
      var_list     = vars,
      indata       = phy,
      dist_obj     = dist_results[[dist_name]],
      permutations = permutations
    )
  })

  names(out) <- names(sig_vars)
  out
}

# 1. get significant variables
sig_vars_age0  <- get_sig_vars(results_PERMANOVA_age0)
sig_vars_age4  <- get_sig_vars(results_PERMANOVA_age4)
sig_vars_age12 <- get_sig_vars(results_PERMANOVA_age12)

# 2. collinearity check per distance metric (Change age from here through multiple PERMANOVA function)
col_check_age12 <- lapply(names(sig_vars_age12), function(dist_name) {
  vars <- sig_vars_age12[[dist_name]]
  
  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("Distance:", dist_name, "\n")
  cat("============================\n")
  
  if (length(vars) < 2) {
    cat("Skipping — fewer than 2 significant variables\n")
    return(list(clean_vars = character(0), removed = character(0)))
  }
  
  check_collinearity(vars, phy_age12)
}) |> setNames(names(sig_vars_age12))

# 3. extract clean vars
clean_sig_vars_age12 <- lapply(col_check_age12, function(x) x$clean_vars)

# 4. run multi-PERMANOVA
multi_results_age12 <- run_multi_permanova(
  sig_vars     = clean_sig_vars_age12,
  phy          = phy_age12,
  dist_results = dist_matrices_age12
)

multi_results_age0[["sorensen"]]
multi_results_age0[["aitchison"]]

multi_results_age4[["sorensen"]]
multi_results_age4[["aitchison"]]

multi_results_age12[["sorensen"]]
multi_results_age12[["aitchison"]]
```


## lollipop plot to display simple and combined results within one plot (From Dr. Rebecca Knoll)

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=7}
perm_result_df$test <- "Multiple PERMANOVA"

tax_bray_sig$test <- "Simple PERMANOVA"

a <- perm_result_df %>% 
  filter(pr_f<=0.05) %>% 
  select(variable, r2, pr_f, N_obs, p_sig, test) %>% 
  rename(n=N_obs) %>% 
  rename(R2=r2) %>% 
  rename(P=pr_f)%>% 
  select(variable, R2, n, P, test) 

b <- tax_bray_sig%>% 
  #rownames_to_column("variable") %>% 
  select(variable, R2, n, P, test) 

df_ab <- rbind(a,b) %>% arrange(desc(R2))

p4 <- df_ab %>% 
  filter(P <= 0.1) %>% 
  mutate(variable_new = fct_reorder(variable, -R2)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = R2 * 100 , y = variable_new, color = test, group = test)) +
  geom_segment(aes(yend = variable_new, xend = 0), size = 1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c( "#765085","#31A1B3FF"), labels=c("Multiple PERMANOVA", "Simple PERMANOVA")) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", text = element_text(size = 14), axis.text = element_text(size = 14)) +
  labs(color = "Test\nType", x = "Explained variance (%)", y = "") #+
  #scale_x_log10()
  # Adjust x-axis limits with expand for padding
  #scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.035, 0.05))) +
  
  
  # Add text labels on top of the bars
  geom_text(aes(label = paste("N=", n), group = test),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),  # Match the dodge width for labels
            hjust = -0.3,  # Positioning above the bar
            size = 4,      # Text size
            color = "black")
p4
```

#Clinical Characteristics Table
```{r}
library(gtsummary)

sam_sub_age4_demotable <- sam_sub_age4
sam_sub_age4_demotable <- sam_sub_age4_demotable %>%
  left_join(
    formatted_merged_df %>% 
      select(Subject_ID, Group, child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1),
    by = c("Subject_ID", "Group")
  )
sam_sub_age4_demotable$child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1 <- sam_sub_age4_demotable$child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1 == 1

sam_sub_age4_demotable <- sam_sub_age4_demotable %>%
  mutate(
    chronic_rhinitis36m_current = factor(
      chronic_rhinitis36m_current,
      levels = c(FALSE, TRUE),
      labels = c("No chronic rhinitis", "Chronic rhinitis")
    )
  )

sam_sub_age4_demotable <- sam_sub_age4_demotable %>%
  mutate(
    race_white = factor(dem_c_4___1, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race_black = factor(dem_c_4___2, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race_american_native = factor(dem_c_4___3, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race_asian_indian = factor(dem_c_4___4, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race_asian = factor(dem_c_4___5, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    #race_pacific_islander = factor(dem_c_4___6, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
    race_other = factor(dem_c_4___7, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )

race_vars <- c("race_white", "race_black", "race_american_native", "race_asian_indian", "race_asian", "race_other")

library(gtsummary)

tbl1 <- sam_sub_age4_demotable %>%
  select(
    chronic_rhinitis36m_current,
    all_of(race_vars),
    sex,
    canoe_site.x,
    child...6.months_ifp_b01,
    pmci_c6___1,
    child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1
  ) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = chronic_rhinitis36m_current,

    type = list(
      all_of(race_vars) ~ "dichotomous",
      canoe_site.x ~ "categorical",
      sex ~ "categorical",
      child...6.months_ifp_b01 ~ "categorical",
      pmci_c6___1 ~ "categorical",
      child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1 ~ "categorical"
    ),

    value = all_of(race_vars) ~ "Yes",

    statistic = all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)",

    label = list(
      race_white   ~ "White",
      race_black   ~ "Black",
      race_american_native ~ "American or Alaskan Native",
      race_asian_indian ~ "Asian Indian",
      race_asian ~ "Asian",
      race_other ~ "Other race",
      sex ~ "Child sex",
      canoe_site.x ~ "Study site",
      child...6.months_ifp_b01 ~ "Infant ever breastfed at 6 months",
      pmci_c6___1 ~ "Infant born vaginally",
      child...18.months_mediec_c_ab_q1 ~ "Infant given antibiotics by 18 months"
    ),

    missing = "no"
  ) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  add_p(
    test = list(
      all_categorical() ~ "chisq.test",
      all_dichotomous() ~ "fisher.test"
    )
  ) %>%
  bold_labels()

tbl1 <- tbl1 %>%
  modify_table_body(
    ~ {
      tb <- .x
      header_row <- tb[1, ]
      header_row[,] <- NA
      header_row$label <- "Race"
      header_row$row_type <- "label"
      bind_rows(header_row, tb)
    }
  ) %>%
  modify_table_styling(
    rows = label == "**Race**",
    text_format = "bold"
  ) %>%
  modify_table_styling(
    rows = row_type == "level" & variable %in% race_vars,
    indent = 2
  )


tbl1 <- tbl1 %>%
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x %>%
      mutate(
        row_type = ifelse(variable %in% race_vars, "level", row_type)
      )
  ) %>%
  modify_table_styling(
    rows = variable %in% race_vars,
    indent = 2
  )

tbl1 <- tbl1 %>%
  modify_caption(
    "**Table 1. Clinical characteristics based off diagnosis of current chronic rhinitis at 36 months**"
  )

tbl1

as_gt(tbl1) %>%
  gtsave("~/Desktop/Table1_clinical_characteristics.png")
```
